telegramText: MARKET WRAP — Apr 25, 2026 Markets displayed classic late-cycle bifurcation as crypto surged on institutional adoption while traditional assets stayed defensive amid Middle East tensions. Bitcoin's institutional flows contrasted with cautious equity positioning as geopolitical risk drove energy security concerns and displacement themes higher. CLOSING PRICES BTC: $77,529 · ETH: $2,317 · SOL: $86.06 · GOLD: $4,715 · SPY: $713.94 (+0.4%) · QQQ: $663.88 (+1.3%) · VIX: $18.71 (-3.1%) · TLT: $86.71 (-0.0%) · DXY: $98.51 (-0.3%) · OIL: $94.4 (-1.5%) EVENING NEWS ▲ XRP Ledger Beats Ethereum in 30-Day Capital Flows With $1.1B in Net Inflows ▲ Altcoin season index rises to 41 – Analyst predicts a 60% upside ▲ Next Crypto to Explode in 2026: Pepeto Tops the Board as Solana Hosts Tokenized SpaceX Sto ▲ Bitcoin dominance rises above 60% – Weak altcoin demand delays rotation ▼ Bitcoin Falls as Trump Cancels U.S. Trip to Pakistan for Iran Peace Talks ▲ Bitcoin Hit Its Highest Price Since January—Why VanEck Analysts See More Potential Gains SCENARIO SHIFTS ▲ Late Cycle: Crypto surge and monetary tightening cycles suggest advanced expansion phase ▼ Goldilocks: Geopolitical tensions and energy price volatility disrupt benign conditions ▲ Stagflation: Energy shock risks and monetary tightening amid growth concerns ▲ Hegemony: US naval expansion and market outperformance vs Europe strengthens dominance ▲ Displacement: Crypto institutional adoption and quantum computing threats accelerate financial transformation REGIME CHECK ▲ Risk-On · Neutral · OVERHEATING · Gauge: 8.9/100 UPCOMING — HIGH IMPACT US New home sales (AAMR)(Mar) — May 5, 02:00 PM UTC (prev: 1.8%) US Trade Balance (billion USD)(Mar) — May 5, 12:30 PM UTC (prev: -57.3) US Durable goods orders (MoM) (Revised) (Mar) — May 4, 02:00 PM UTC EU Markit Manufacturing Purchasing Managers Index (Final)(Apr) — May 4, 08:00 AM UTC (prev: 52.2) US ISM Manufacturing Purchasing Managers Index(Apr) — May 1, 02:00 PM UTC (prev: 52.7) Producer Price Index (YoY)(Q1) — May 1, 01:30 AM UTC (prev: 3.5%) CONTRARIAN • Bitcoin's crisis rally may be liquidity-driven rather than safe haven adoption, vulnerable to revers • Energy security fears are overblown given military escort availability and alternative supply routes agentcanary.ai | Not financial advice
